multiplechoiceresponse.label: Why does Lecture 2 propose training a classification tree after the fairness-aware optimization?

choicegroup.choice: To improve the predictive accuracy of the original classifier
To obtain an interpretable summary of which observations were affected by label flipping
To remove protected attributes from the dataset

div.p: The lecture emphasizes interpretability after optimization, not during it. The mixed-integer program determines which labels to flip, but does not explain why those flips occur. Training a classification tree on the flip/no-flip labels provides insight into the characteristics associated with fairness adjustments. This makes the intervention transparent and auditable.
